title: cayratia acuminata
intro: Cayratia acuminata, also known as the wild grape or five-angle vine, is a perennial climbing plant that belongs to the family Vitaceae. The medicinal properties of Cayratia acuminata lie in its leaves, which have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cayratia acuminata include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to help in reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In terms of herbal preparations, Cayratia acuminata leaves are often made into a decoction or a paste, which can be applied topically or consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
